title: Command Packaging, Build Identity, Cross-Platform Behavior, and Distribution · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers command packaging, build identity, cross-platform behavior, and distribution into a changed reproducible cross-platform Go Pokedex artifact. steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Command Package Lay…
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 1273 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind reviewed source to cross-platform builds, inspected archives, checksums, and installed smoke behavior; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep cmd entrypoint thin, internal packages directional, fixtures separate, and runtime paths independent of source checkout.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Pokedex Go Install Upgrade…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-install-upgrade-uninstall-1bfop2" that uses case 2867 to bind reviewed source to cross-platform builds, inspected archives, checksums, and installed smoke behavior. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
